items.title: Reuters: Intel and AMD are pursuing long-term server CPU supply agreements with major Chinese server customers in response to surging prices and tightening supply. The proposed agreements, ranging from one to more than two years, would primarily guarantee purchase volumes while allowing prices to adjust in line with market conditions. In addition to one-year contracts, discussions are also underway for agreements extending beyond two years. In China’s market, prices for some server CPUs have risen more than 10% month over month, with certain products up over 40% since the beginning of the year. Lead times for some of Intel’s server CPU products have stretched to as long as six months, unders
items.content:
Reuters: Intel and AMD are pursuing long-term server CPU supply agreements with major Chinese server customers in response to surging prices and tightening supply.

The proposed agreements, ranging from one to more than two years, would primarily guarantee purchase volumes while allowing prices to adjust in line with market conditions. In addition to one-year contracts, discussions are also underway for agreements extending beyond two years.

In China’s market, prices for some server CPUs have risen more than 10% month over month, with certain products up over 40% since the beginning of the year.

Lead times for some of Intel’s server CPU products have stretched to as long as six months, underscoring the growing supply-demand imbalance.
— Jukan (@jukan05) Jul 23, 2026

items.title: Samsung Electro-Mechanics announced an MLCC supply agreement today, and what stands out is that it identified the counterparty only as a “large global company.” You know what that implies: MLCCs are now becoming subject to LTAs with Big Tech companies as well.
items.content:
Samsung Electro-Mechanics announced an MLCC supply agreement today, and what stands out is that it identified the counterparty only as a “large global company.”

You know what that implies: MLCCs are now becoming subject to LTAs with Big Tech companies as well.
— Jukan (@jukan05) Jul 23, 2026

items.content:
如果google的2027年11m TPU产能只有3m是给anthropic等客户，其他都是自己建DC = google 自建8m(10GW)，2GW GPU

但明年实际能online芯片和明年芯片产能，有一个季度的时间差

items.title: BofA’s Vivek Arya made an excellent point, which I’d like to share. Is open-source AI bearish for memory? Closed models amortize global demand across shared HBM pools concentrated in a handful of data centers, whereas open models create a new memory footprint with every deployment. If 10,000 companies self-host the same open model, the model weights must be replicated across 10,000 separate HBM pools, with each deployment also requiring its own KV cache. As 128K–1M token contexts become commonplace in 2026, the KV cache alone can exceed 40GB per active session. Low-cost Chinese APIs drive greater inference demand, broader enterprise self-hosting, and more memory sockets worldwide. In short,
items.content:
BofA’s Vivek Arya made an excellent point, which I’d like to share.

Is open-source AI bearish for memory?

Closed models amortize global demand across shared HBM pools concentrated in a handful of data centers, whereas open models create a new memory footprint with every deployment. If 10,000 companies self-host the same open model, the model weights must be replicated across 10,000 separate HBM pools, with each deployment also requiring its own KV cache. As 128K–1M token contexts become commonplace in 2026, the KV cache alone can exceed 40GB per active session.

Low-cost Chinese APIs drive greater inference demand, broader enterprise self-hosting, and more memory sockets worldwide. In short, closed models concentrate memory demand, while open models multiply it.
— Jukan (@jukan05) Jul 22, 2026

items.content:
What the actual fuck are you talking about?

You seem to be under the delusion that I deliberately spread malicious rumors about Intel with some kind of ulterior motive, but:

1. This isn’t even negative news about Intel in the first place.
2. The reason Intel is down right now is because the Korean stock market is going through a correction.
3. I’m not the original source of this report. It came from the Korean media, and plenty of other people had already reposted it before I did.

I genuinely have no idea what kind of batshit logic leads you to conclude that I crashed Intel’s stock by spreading malicious rumors. Intel is my second-largest position.

P.S. I’ve been feeling really drained lately. There are simply too many people making baseless accusations. Some claim that I’m being paid by a hedge fund to manipulate stock prices, while others accuse me of spreading malicious rumors even though I wasn’t the original source and merely reposted a news report. Honestly, I’m becoming deeply frustrated with this platform.Lisan Al Pablo (@Pablo01618)That rumor actually hurt  $intc   @jukan05 the fuck are you doing this isn’t the first time your dumbasses have done shit like this your gonna catch a case—  https://x.com/Pablo01618/status/2079808355883688246 
— Jukan (@jukan05) Jul 22, 2026

items.title: Official statement from SK hynix: This disclosure is issued to clarify the article titled “SK hynix’s Bold Move… to Acquire Intel’s Ohio Plant in the U.S.,” published by the JoongAng Ilbo on July 22, 2026. The Company continuously reviews various investment and acquisition opportunities as part of its business activities. However, contrary to the article, the Company has neither pursued nor decided to acquire Intel’s Ohio site and fab. Disclosure Officer: Kim Woo-hyun, Head of Finance Division
items.content:
Official statement from SK hynix:

This disclosure is issued to clarify the article titled “SK hynix’s Bold Move… to Acquire Intel’s Ohio Plant in the U.S.,” published by the JoongAng Ilbo on July 22, 2026.

The Company continuously reviews various investment and acquisition opportunities as part of its business activities.

However, contrary to the article, the Company has neither pursued nor decided to acquire Intel’s Ohio site and fab.

Disclosure Officer: Kim Woo-hyun, Head of Finance Division
— Jukan (@jukan05) Jul 22, 2026

items.title: Intel denied that it was selling its Ohio fab. SK hynix denied that it was acquiring the Ohio fab. However, neither company denied the possibility of a joint venture between them—they simply did not address it.
items.content:

SK hynix denied that it was acquiring the Ohio fab.
